description: Heart-healthy diet rich in fruits, vegetables, whole grains, olive oil, and lean proteins from the Mediterranean region

med_greek_yogurt_parfait.name: Greek Yogurt Parfait with Honey and Walnuts
med_greek_yogurt_parfait.description: Creamy Greek yogurt layered with golden honey, crunchy walnuts, and fresh mixed berries
instructions: Spoon half the Greek yogurt into a bowl or glass.
Layer with half the berries and a drizzle of honey.
Add the remaining yogurt, top with remaining berries, walnuts, and flaxseed.
Drizzle with the rest of the honey and serve immediately.

med_shakshuka.name: Shakshuka with Crusty Bread
med_shakshuka.description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce, served with warm crusty whole wheat bread for dipping
instructions: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Saute onion and bell pepper until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic, cumin, paprika, and red pepper flakes. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in crushed tomatoes, stir well, and simmer for 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Make two wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each. Cover and cook for 6-8 minutes until egg whites are set.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve with crusty bread for dipping.

med_avocado_egg_toast.name: Mediterranean Avocado Toast with Poached Egg
med_avocado_egg_toast.description: Whole grain toast topped with mashed avocado, a perfectly poached egg, cherry tomatoes, and a sprinkle of za'atar
ingredients: Pinch of sea salt and black pepper
instructions: Toast the whole grain bread until golden and crisp.
Mash the avocado with lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Spread onto toast slices.
Bring a small pot of water to a gentle simmer, add vinegar, and poach the egg for 3-4 minutes.
Top toast with halved cherry tomatoes and the poached egg.
Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with za'atar seasoning.

med_spinach_feta_omelette.name: Spinach and Feta Omelette
med_spinach_feta_omelette.description: Fluffy omelette stuffed with wilted baby spinach, crumbled feta cheese, and sun-dried tomatoes
instructions: Whisk eggs with a pinch of salt, pepper, and oregano.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Pour in eggs and cook until edges begin to set, about 2 minutes.
Add spinach, feta, and sun-dried tomatoes to one half of the omelette.
Fold the omelette over, cook 1 more minute, and slide onto a plate.

med_overnight_oats.name: Mediterranean Overnight Oats
med_overnight_oats.description: Creamy overnight oats with figs, pistachios, and a drizzle of honey, inspired by the flavors of Greece
instructions: Combine oats, Greek yogurt, almond milk, chia seeds, and cinnamon in a jar or container.
Stir well, cover, and refrigerate overnight or at least 6 hours.
In the morning, top with chopped figs and pistachios.
Drizzle with honey and enjoy cold or at room temperature.

med_turkish_egg_bowl.name: Turkish Eggs (Cilbir)
med_turkish_egg_bowl.description: Soft poached eggs nestled in garlicky yogurt, topped with Aleppo pepper butter and fresh dill
instructions: Mix Greek yogurt with minced garlic and a pinch of salt. Spread onto a shallow bowl.
Bring water to a gentle simmer and poach eggs for 3-4 minutes until whites are set.
Place poached eggs on top of the yogurt.
Melt butter in a small pan, add Aleppo pepper, and swirl for 30 seconds.
Drizzle the pepper butter over the eggs, garnish with dill, and serve with warm pita.

med_fig_ricotta_toast.name: Fig and Ricotta Toast with Honey
med_fig_ricotta_toast.description: Toasted whole grain bread with creamy ricotta, sliced fresh figs, honey, and a scattering of toasted almonds
instructions: Toast whole grain bread until golden.
Spread ricotta evenly over each slice.
Arrange fig slices on top of the ricotta.
Sprinkle with toasted almonds, drizzle with honey, add a pinch of sea salt, and garnish with mint.

med_veggie_frittata.name: Mediterranean Vegetable Frittata
med_veggie_frittata.description: Oven-baked egg frittata loaded with zucchini, roasted red peppers, Kalamata olives, and herbs
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Heat olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium heat.
Saute zucchini for 3 minutes until lightly golden.
Whisk eggs with thyme, salt, and pepper. Pour over zucchini.
Scatter roasted red peppers, olives, and feta over the top.
Cook on stovetop for 2 minutes, then transfer to oven and bake 12-15 minutes until set.
Let cool slightly, garnish with basil, and slice into wedges.

med_whole_wheat_pancakes.name: Whole Wheat Pancakes with Orange and Olive Oil
med_whole_wheat_pancakes.description: Light and fluffy whole wheat pancakes infused with fresh orange zest, drizzled with olive oil and topped with citrus segments
instructions: Whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.
In another bowl, beat egg with milk, olive oil, honey, and orange zest.
Combine wet and dry ingredients, stirring until just mixed (a few lumps are fine).
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat. Pour 1/4 cup batter per pancake.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface, flip, and cook another 1-2 minutes.
Serve topped with orange segments and an extra drizzle of honey.

med_labneh_bowl.name: Labneh Breakfast Bowl
med_labneh_bowl.description: Thick strained yogurt (labneh) served with olive oil, za'atar, cucumber, tomato, and warm flatbread
instructions: Spread labneh in a shallow bowl, creating a slight well in the center.
Drizzle generously with olive oil and sprinkle za'atar over the top.
Arrange diced cucumber and cherry tomatoes around the bowl.
Garnish with fresh mint and a pinch of sea salt.
Serve with warm pita bread torn into pieces for scooping.

med_banana_walnut_oatmeal.name: Warm Oatmeal with Banana, Walnuts, and Cinnamon
med_banana_walnut_oatmeal.description: Hearty steel-cut oats cooked with sliced banana, toasted walnuts, a touch of cinnamon, and a drizzle of tahini
instructions: Bring water and milk to a boil in a saucepan. Add oats and reduce to a simmer.
Cook for 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until oats are tender and creamy.
Pour into a bowl and top with sliced banana and chopped walnuts.
Drizzle with tahini and honey, and dust with cinnamon.

med_smoked_salmon_plate.name: Smoked Salmon Breakfast Plate
med_smoked_salmon_plate.description: Silky smoked salmon with cream cheese, capers, red onion, and whole grain crackers — an elegant morning spread
instructions: Arrange smoked salmon slices on a plate.
Place small dollops of cream cheese alongside the salmon.
Scatter capers and red onion slices over the plate.
Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the salmon and garnish with dill.
Serve with whole grain crackers on the side.

med_greek_salad.name: Classic Greek Salad
med_greek_salad.description: Crisp cucumbers, ripe tomatoes, red onion, Kalamata olives, and feta cheese dressed in olive oil and red wine vinegar
instructions: Combine romaine, cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, and olives in a large bowl.
Whisk together olive oil, red wine vinegar, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad and toss gently.
Top with cubed feta cheese and serve immediately.

med_lentil_soup.name: Red Lentil Soup with Lemon
med_lentil_soup.description: Velvety Turkish-style red lentil soup finished with a bright squeeze of lemon and a swirl of olive oil
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Saute onion and carrot for 5 minutes.
Add garlic, cumin, and turmeric. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add lentils and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes until lentils are very soft.
Use an immersion blender to partially puree the soup (leave some texture).
Stir in lemon juice, season with salt and pepper, and serve with a drizzle of olive oil and parsley.

med_grilled_chicken_wrap.name: Grilled Chicken and Hummus Wrap
med_grilled_chicken_wrap.description: Warm whole wheat wrap filled with seasoned grilled chicken, creamy hummus, crunchy vegetables, and fresh herbs
instructions: Season chicken breast with za'atar, salt, and pepper. Brush with olive oil.
Grill or pan-sear chicken over medium-high heat for 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through (165°F internal).
Let chicken rest for 3 minutes, then slice into strips.
Spread hummus across the center of the tortilla.
Layer chicken strips, lettuce, cucumber, tomatoes, and pickled onion on top.
Fold the sides in and roll tightly into a wrap.

med_tuna_nicoise.name: Tuna Nicoise Salad
med_tuna_nicoise.description: A French Riviera classic with seared tuna, green beans, boiled egg, olives, and potatoes in a Dijon vinaigrette
instructions: Boil potatoes until fork-tender, about 10 minutes. Blanch green beans for 3 minutes. Drain and cool.
Season tuna with salt and pepper. Sear in a hot skillet for 2 minutes per side for medium-rare.
Whisk together Dijon mustard, olive oil, red wine vinegar, salt, and pepper for the dressing.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate. Top with sliced potatoes, green beans, tomatoes, olives, and halved egg.
Slice tuna and place on top. Drizzle with vinaigrette.

med_falafel_bowl.name: Falafel Bowl with Tahini Sauce
med_falafel_bowl.description: Crispy baked falafel served over a bed of fluffy couscous with pickled turnips, fresh vegetables, and creamy tahini drizzle
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Pulse chickpeas, parsley, garlic, cumin, and salt in a food processor until a coarse paste forms.
Shape mixture into 8 small patties. Place on a lined baking sheet and brush with olive oil.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and crisp.
Prepare couscous according to package directions and fluff with a fork.
Whisk tahini with lemon juice and water to make a drizzle sauce.
Assemble bowl with couscous, 4 falafel, cucumber, tomato, and tahini sauce.

med_caprese_panini.name: Caprese Panini
med_caprese_panini.description: Pressed sandwich with fresh mozzarella, ripe tomato, basil leaves, and a balsamic glaze on ciabatta
ingredients: Pinch of sea salt and black pepper
instructions: Brush the cut sides of the ciabatta with olive oil.
Layer mozzarella, tomato slices, and basil on the bottom half.
Season with salt and pepper, drizzle with balsamic glaze, and place the top on.
Press in a panini press or grill pan with a heavy skillet on top for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and cheese melts.

med_chicken_souvlaki_plate.name: Chicken Souvlaki Plate
med_chicken_souvlaki_plate.description: Herb-marinated grilled chicken skewers served with warm pita, tzatziki, and a bright tomato-cucumber salad
ingredients: 6 oz boneless skinless chicken breast, cubed
instructions: Marinate chicken cubes in olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, garlic, salt, and pepper for at least 10 minutes.
Thread chicken onto skewers and grill or pan-sear over medium-high heat for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally.
Warm the pita in the pan for 30 seconds per side.
Toss tomato, cucumber, and red onion with a squeeze of lemon for a quick salad.
Serve skewers with pita, tzatziki, and the side salad.

med_white_bean_tuna_salad.name: Tuscan White Bean and Tuna Salad
med_white_bean_tuna_salad.description: Hearty Italian-inspired salad of cannellini beans, olive oil-packed tuna, red onion, and fresh herbs in a lemon dressing
ingredients: 1 can (5 oz) tuna in olive oil, drained
instructions: Combine cannellini beans, tuna, red onion, celery, and capers in a bowl.
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing over the bean mixture and toss gently.
Serve over a bed of arugula and garnish with parsley.

med_stuffed_pepper_halves.name: Quinoa-Stuffed Bell Pepper Halves
med_stuffed_pepper_halves.description: Colorful bell peppers filled with herbed quinoa, chickpeas, sun-dried tomatoes, and topped with crumbled feta
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Place pepper halves cut-side up on a baking sheet.
Mix cooked quinoa with chickpeas, sun-dried tomatoes, parsley, olive oil, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Divide the quinoa mixture evenly between the pepper halves.
Top with crumbled feta cheese.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until peppers are tender and filling is heated through.

med_fattoush_salad.name: Fattoush Salad with Sumac Dressing
med_fattoush_salad.description: Crunchy Lebanese bread salad with toasted pita chips, radishes, fresh herbs, and a tangy sumac vinaigrette
ingredients: 1 whole wheat pita, torn into pieces
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Spread pita pieces on a baking sheet and toast for 6-8 minutes until crisp.
Combine romaine, cucumber, tomato, radishes, green onions, mint, and parsley in a large bowl.
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, sumac, and salt for the dressing.
Add toasted pita chips to the salad and drizzle with dressing.
Toss gently and serve immediately while pita is still crunchy.

med_sardine_toast.name: Sardine Toast with Lemon and Arugula
med_sardine_toast.description: Mediterranean-style open-faced toast topped with olive oil sardines, peppery arugula, and a squeeze of lemon
ingredients: 1 can (3.75 oz) sardines in olive oil
instructions: Toast the sourdough slices until golden and crisp.
Spread a thin layer of Dijon mustard on each slice.
Arrange sardines on top of the toast, gently pressing with a fork.
Top with arugula, red onion slices, and a squeeze of lemon.
Finish with cracked black pepper and red pepper flakes.

med_grilled_halloumi_salad.name: Grilled Halloumi and Watermelon Salad
med_grilled_halloumi_salad.description: A summery combination of seared halloumi cheese and juicy watermelon with mint, arugula, and balsamic reduction
ingredients: 3 oz halloumi cheese, sliced into 1/4-inch planks
instructions: Heat a non-stick skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Sear halloumi slices for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown.
Arrange arugula on a plate and scatter watermelon cubes over the top.
Place grilled halloumi on the salad.
Drizzle with olive oil and balsamic glaze, scatter mint leaves, and season with salt and pepper.

med_grilled_salmon.name: Herb-Crusted Grilled Salmon
med_grilled_salmon.description: Wild-caught salmon fillet crusted with fresh herbs, garlic, and lemon, served alongside roasted asparagus
instructions: Preheat grill or oven to 400°F. Mix garlic, dill, parsley, 1 tbsp olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Pat salmon dry and spread the herb mixture over the top.
Toss asparagus with remaining olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Grill salmon skin-side down for 12-15 minutes (or bake) until it flakes easily.
Grill asparagus alongside for 6-8 minutes, turning once.
Serve salmon with asparagus and lemon wedges.

med_chicken_orzo.name: Lemon Chicken with Orzo and Spinach
med_chicken_orzo.description: Tender pan-seared chicken thighs with lemony orzo pasta, wilted spinach, and a scattering of toasted pine nuts
instructions: Cook orzo according to package directions. Drain and set aside.
Season chicken thigh with salt, pepper, and lemon zest. Sear in 1 tbsp olive oil for 5-6 minutes per side. Set aside to rest.
In the same pan, add remaining olive oil and garlic. Cook 30 seconds.
Add spinach, chicken broth, and lemon juice. Stir until spinach wilts.
Toss in cooked orzo and pine nuts. Slice chicken and serve on top.

med_shrimp_pasta.name: Garlic Shrimp Pasta with Cherry Tomatoes
med_shrimp_pasta.description: Al dente whole wheat linguine tossed with sauteed garlic shrimp, blistered cherry tomatoes, white wine, and fresh basil
ingredients: 5 oz large shrimp, peeled and deveined
instructions: Cook linguine according to package directions until al dente. Reserve 1/4 cup pasta water before draining.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Add garlic and red pepper flakes, cook 30 seconds.
Add shrimp and cook 2-3 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
Add cherry tomatoes to the skillet and cook until they blister, about 3 minutes. Add white wine and simmer 1 minute.
Toss in pasta, shrimp, and a splash of pasta water. Stir to combine.
Serve topped with fresh basil and Parmesan.

med_lamb_kofta.name: Lamb Kofta with Yogurt Sauce
med_lamb_kofta.description: Spiced ground lamb kebabs grilled to perfection, served with cooling yogurt sauce and a tabbouleh side
ingredients: 1/2 cup chopped parsley and tomato (for tabbouleh)
instructions: Mix ground lamb with grated onion, garlic, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, parsley, salt, and pepper.
Shape the mixture around skewers into elongated oval shapes.
Grill or pan-sear kofta over medium-high heat for 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through.
Mix yogurt with lemon juice and a pinch of salt for the sauce.
Toss cooked bulgur with chopped parsley, tomato, and a drizzle of olive oil.
Serve kofta with tabbouleh and yogurt sauce.

med_baked_cod.name: Baked Cod with Olive Tapenade
med_baked_cod.description: Flaky white cod baked under a savory olive tapenade crust, served with roasted cherry tomatoes and green beans
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Place cod on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Spread olive tapenade evenly over the top of the cod.
Arrange cherry tomatoes and green beans around the fish. Toss vegetables with olive oil, garlic, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Bake for 18-20 minutes until cod flakes easily and vegetables are tender.
Serve with lemon wedges.

med_eggplant_parmesan.name: Lightened Eggplant Parmesan
med_eggplant_parmesan.description: Baked (not fried) eggplant rounds layered with marinara, fresh mozzarella, and Parmesan, served with a side salad
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Salt eggplant slices and let sit 10 minutes, then pat dry.
Dip eggplant rounds in beaten egg, then coat with panko breadcrumbs.
Place on an oiled baking sheet and bake for 20 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden.
Layer baked eggplant in a baking dish with marinara and mozzarella slices.
Sprinkle with Parmesan and bake 10-12 more minutes until cheese is bubbly.
Garnish with fresh basil and serve.

med_moroccan_chicken.name: Moroccan Chicken Tagine
med_moroccan_chicken.description: Aromatic braised chicken with preserved lemons, green olives, and warm spices over fluffy couscous
ingredients: 1/2 preserved lemon, pulp discarded, rind chopped (or zest of 1 lemon)
instructions: Season chicken with ginger, turmeric, cinnamon, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a tagine or Dutch oven. Brown chicken on both sides, about 3 minutes per side. Remove.
Saute onion until softened. Add garlic and cook 30 seconds.
Return chicken to the pot, add broth, preserved lemon, and olives. Cover and simmer 30 minutes.
Prepare couscous according to package directions.
Serve chicken and sauce over couscous, garnished with cilantro.

med_pasta_puttanesca.name: Pasta Puttanesca
med_pasta_puttanesca.description: Bold and briny whole wheat spaghetti with tomatoes, capers, Kalamata olives, anchovies, and garlic
instructions: Cook spaghetti according to package directions. Reserve 1/4 cup pasta water.
Heat olive oil in a large pan. Add garlic, anchovies, and red pepper flakes. Cook until anchovies dissolve.
Add diced tomatoes, olives, and capers. Simmer 12-15 minutes until sauce thickens.
Toss drained pasta with the sauce, adding pasta water as needed for consistency.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve.

med_stuffed_grape_leaves.name: Stuffed Grape Leaves (Dolma) with Rice
med_stuffed_grape_leaves.description: Tender grape leaves filled with herbed rice, pine nuts, and currants, braised in lemon broth
instructions: Saute onion in olive oil until soft. Add rice, pine nuts, currants, dill, mint, salt, and pepper. Stir for 1 minute.
Place a grape leaf flat, vein-side up. Add 1 tbsp filling near the stem end.
Fold sides inward, then roll tightly from stem to tip. Repeat with remaining leaves.
Arrange rolls seam-side down in a pot. Add lemon juice and water.
Place a plate on top to keep them submerged. Cover and simmer 40-45 minutes until rice is cooked.
Let cool slightly and serve warm or at room temperature.

med_sea_bass.name: Pan-Seared Sea Bass with Caponata
med_sea_bass.description: Crispy-skinned sea bass served over Sicilian caponata - a sweet and sour eggplant relish with tomatoes and capers
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet. Saute eggplant for 5 minutes until golden. Add celery and onion, cook 3 minutes.
Add tomato, capers, olives, vinegar, and honey. Simmer 10 minutes until thickened. Season and set aside.
Pat sea bass dry, season with salt and pepper. Heat remaining olive oil in a clean skillet over medium-high heat.
Place fish skin-side down and cook 4 minutes until skin is crispy. Flip and cook 2-3 minutes more.
Spoon caponata onto a plate, place sea bass on top, and garnish with basil.

med_chicken_shawarma_bowl.name: Chicken Shawarma Rice Bowl
med_chicken_shawarma_bowl.description: Warmly spiced chicken shawarma over brown rice with pickled onions, fresh herbs, and garlic sauce
ingredients: 6 oz boneless skinless chicken thighs, sliced
instructions: Mix cumin, turmeric, paprika, cinnamon, salt, and pepper. Toss chicken slices with the spice mix and olive oil.
Sear chicken in a hot skillet for 4-5 minutes per side until cooked through and slightly charred.
Mix Greek yogurt with garlic, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt for the sauce.
Assemble bowl with brown rice, sliced chicken, pickled onions, and cucumber.
Drizzle garlic yogurt sauce over the top and garnish with parsley.

med_mushroom_risotto.name: Wild Mushroom Risotto
med_mushroom_risotto.description: Creamy arborio rice slowly cooked with a medley of wild mushrooms, finished with Parmesan and fresh thyme
ingredients: 1.5 cups mixed mushrooms (cremini, shiitake, oyster), sliced
instructions: Heat olive oil in a wide pan. Saute mushrooms until golden, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add butter and saute onion until translucent. Add garlic, cook 30 seconds.
Add arborio rice and stir for 1 minute until coated. Pour in wine and stir until absorbed.
Add warm broth one ladle at a time, stirring frequently, waiting for each addition to be absorbed before adding the next (about 18-20 minutes).
Stir in mushrooms, Parmesan, and thyme. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve immediately while creamy.

med_grilled_swordfish.name: Grilled Swordfish with Salsa Verde
med_grilled_swordfish.description: Meaty swordfish steak grilled and topped with a vibrant Italian salsa verde of parsley, capers, and anchovy
instructions: Make salsa verde: pulse parsley, capers, anchovy, garlic, 2 tbsp olive oil, and vinegar in a mini food processor until chunky.
Brush swordfish with remaining olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill over medium-high heat for 4-5 minutes per side until firm and opaque.
Serve swordfish with a generous spoonful of salsa verde and roasted zucchini on the side.

med_spanakopita_bake.name: Spanakopita (Greek Spinach Pie)
med_spanakopita_bake.description: Layers of flaky phyllo pastry encasing a savory filling of spinach, feta, and dill - a Greek taverna classic
ingredients: 2 tbsp extra virgin olive oil (for brushing)
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Wilt spinach in a pan, squeeze out excess water, and let cool.
Mix spinach with feta, ricotta, egg, green onions, dill, nutmeg, salt, and pepper.
Layer 2 sheets of phyllo in a small baking dish, brushing each with olive oil.
Spread the spinach filling evenly over the phyllo.
Top with remaining 2 phyllo sheets, brushing each with olive oil. Tuck edges under.
Score the top into portions and bake 30-35 minutes until golden and crisp.

med_hummus_plate.name: Loaded Hummus Plate
med_hummus_plate.description: Silky homemade hummus topped with spiced chickpeas, pine nuts, and olive oil, served with warm pita and crudites
ingredients: 1/2 whole wheat pita, cut into triangles
instructions: Spread hummus in a shallow bowl, creating a well in the center.
Top with roasted chickpeas and toasted pine nuts.
Drizzle with olive oil and dust with paprika.
Arrange pita triangles and crudites around the plate for dipping.

med_mixed_nuts_dried_fruit.name: Mediterranean Trail Mix
med_mixed_nuts_dried_fruit.description: A satisfying handful of roasted almonds, pistachios, walnuts, dried apricots, and dark chocolate chips
instructions: Combine all nuts, dried apricots, and chocolate chips in a small container.
Sprinkle with a pinch of sea salt.
Toss gently and enjoy as a portable snack.

med_stuffed_dates.name: Almond-Stuffed Dates with Dark Chocolate
med_stuffed_dates.description: Plump Medjool dates filled with marcona almonds and drizzled with melted dark chocolate - a sweet Mediterranean treat
instructions: Pit each date and press an almond into the center.
Melt dark chocolate in a microwave in 15-second intervals, stirring between each.
Drizzle melted chocolate over the stuffed dates.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt and let chocolate set for 5 minutes before serving.

med_tzatziki_veggies.name: Tzatziki with Fresh Vegetable Sticks
med_tzatziki_veggies.description: Cool and creamy cucumber-yogurt dip paired with an assortment of colorful fresh vegetable crudites
instructions: Grate cucumber and squeeze out excess moisture with a clean towel.
Mix yogurt, cucumber, garlic, olive oil, dill, lemon juice, and salt.
Refrigerate for at least 15 minutes to let flavors meld.
Serve in a bowl surrounded by carrot sticks, bell pepper slices, and cucumber rounds.

med_marinated_olives.name: Warm Marinated Olives with Herbs
med_marinated_olives.description: A bowl of mixed olives gently warmed with citrus zest, rosemary, garlic, and red pepper flakes
instructions: Combine olives, olive oil, citrus zests, rosemary, garlic, and red pepper flakes in a small saucepan.
Warm over low heat for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until fragrant.
Transfer to a small serving bowl and enjoy warm or at room temperature.

med_bruschetta.name: Classic Tomato Bruschetta
med_bruschetta.description: Crispy toasted baguette slices topped with a fresh tomato, basil, and garlic mixture drizzled with balsamic vinegar
ingredients: 1 clove garlic (one for rubbing, one minced)
Pinch of sea salt and black pepper
instructions: Toast baguette slices until golden and crisp.
Rub each slice with a cut garlic clove.
Mix diced tomatoes with minced garlic, basil, olive oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Spoon the tomato mixture onto the toasted bread and serve immediately.

med_labneh_balls.name: Labneh Balls in Olive Oil and Herbs
med_labneh_balls.description: Tangy strained yogurt rolled into balls, preserved in olive oil with dried herbs, served with crackers
instructions: Roll chilled labneh into small balls (about 1 inch each).
Place in a small bowl and cover with olive oil.
Sprinkle with za'atar, dried mint, and Aleppo pepper.
Serve with whole grain crackers for spreading.

med_roasted_chickpeas.name: Spiced Roasted Chickpeas
med_roasted_chickpeas.description: Crispy oven-roasted chickpeas seasoned with cumin, smoked paprika, and a touch of cayenne - addictively crunchy
ingredients: 1 can (15 oz) chickpeas, drained and patted very dry
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Pat chickpeas thoroughly dry with paper towels.
Toss chickpeas with olive oil, paprika, cumin, garlic powder, cayenne, and salt.
Spread in a single layer on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Roast for 25-30 minutes, shaking the pan halfway through, until crispy.
Let cool for 5 minutes (they crisp up further as they cool). Store in an open container.

med_caprese_skewers.name: Caprese Skewers with Balsamic Glaze
med_caprese_skewers.description: Mini skewers of cherry tomatoes, fresh mozzarella balls, and basil leaves drizzled with aged balsamic
ingredients: Pinch of sea salt and black pepper
instructions: Thread one basil leaf, one mozzarella ball, and one cherry tomato onto each skewer.
Arrange on a plate and drizzle with olive oil and balsamic glaze.
Season with sea salt and freshly cracked pepper.
Serve immediately as an elegant appetizer or snack.

med_fig_walnut_bites.name: Fig and Walnut Energy Bites
med_fig_walnut_bites.description: No-bake energy bites made with dried figs, walnuts, oats, and a hint of cinnamon - perfect for on-the-go fuel
instructions: Place figs, walnuts, oats, honey, cinnamon, and salt in a food processor.
Pulse until the mixture forms a sticky, cohesive dough.
Roll into 4 small balls (about 1 tablespoon each).
Roll each ball in shredded coconut.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving. Store in the fridge for up to 1 week.

med_tabbouleh_bowl.name: Tabbouleh Grain Bowl with Grilled Vegetables
med_tabbouleh_bowl.description: Vibrant Lebanese tabbouleh with extra bulgur, charred zucchini, eggplant, and a tahini-lemon drizzle
ingredients: 1 cup fresh flat-leaf parsley, finely chopped
instructions: Soak bulgur in boiling water for 15 minutes, then drain and fluff with a fork.
Brush zucchini and eggplant slices with 1 tbsp olive oil. Grill or pan-sear for 3-4 minutes per side until charred.
Toss bulgur with parsley, mint, tomatoes, green onions, remaining olive oil, and half the lemon juice.
Whisk tahini with remaining lemon juice and 1 tbsp water for the drizzle.
Arrange tabbouleh in a bowl, top with grilled vegetables, and drizzle with tahini sauce.

med_olive_oil_cake_bites.name: Mini Olive Oil and Citrus Cake Bites
med_olive_oil_cake_bites.description: Tender little cakes made with extra virgin olive oil, fresh orange juice, and almond flour - a wholesome Mediterranean sweet
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a mini muffin tin or line with papers.
Whisk together almond flour, whole wheat flour, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, beat egg with olive oil, orange juice, honey, and orange zest.
Fold wet ingredients into dry until just combined.
Divide batter among 4 mini muffin cups. Bake 16-18 minutes until golden and a toothpick comes out clean.
Let cool and dust lightly with powdered sugar if desired.
